title: 10 Predictions About the Future of Movies. Plus: Ron Howard!
format: A mailbag on what movies become after COVID, an Olivia de Havilland remembrance, and Sean's talk with Ron Howard about the fire documentary Rebuilding Paradise.

segments.heading: In memoriam
segments.note: Olivia de Havilland and John Saxon

films.title: The Heiress
films.note: Sean's de Havilland recommendation: William Wyler's take on Henry James's Washington Square, recently reissued by Criterion.

films.title: Gone with the Wind
films.note: She was the last major surviving star of the golden-age cast.

films.title: Captain Blood
films.note: One of her pairings with Errol Flynn.

films.title: The Adventures of Robin Hood
films.note: Another Flynn vehicle from her early Warner Bros. run.

films.title: Enter the Dragon
films.note: Sean's shout-out to the late John Saxon, a great character actor.

films.title: Tenebrae
films.note: Saxon in Dario Argento's giallo.

films.title: Joe Kidd
films.note: Saxon opposite Clint Eastwood.

films.title: A Nightmare on Elm Street
films.note: The face you know even if you never knew the name.

segments.heading: The future-of-movies mailbag
segments.note: listener questions on what the movies even are now

films.title: Tenet
films.note: The recurring subject: fourth date change, going international August 26th before a U.S. Labor Day window that nobody trusts. Sean and Amanda war-game a road trip to Vancouver to see it, then remember the border is closed.

films.title: The New Mutants
films.note: The running gag: 'death, taxes, and The New Mutants is never coming out.' A Fox holdover whose HBO contract blocks a Disney+ or PVOD dodge.

films.title: The French Dispatch
films.note: Another Fox-era holdover; Wes Anderson likely has a theatrical clause, so undated rather than PVOD.

films.title: Palm Springs
films.note: The counterexample they both loved: a well-received film with a slow-burn, less front-loaded rollout, though not a $100M opening.

films.title: The Last Duel
films.note: Sean's example of an original, star-driven, $100M-capable movie that just quietly slipped to fall 2021.

films.title: Trolls World Tour
films.note: Universal's PVOD experiment, cited as maybe a predictor, maybe just cutting losses.

films.title: The Rental
films.note: Dave Franco's directorial debut opened on ~420 drive-in screens and did decent business.

films.title: The King of Staten Island
films.note: Universal sent it straight to PVOD.

films.title: Scoob!
films.note: Warner Bros. moved it to PVOD, then HBO Max within six weeks.

films.title: The Trial of the Chicago Seven
films.note: Aaron Sorkin's Netflix film: 'the most Oscar movie I've ever seen,' part of the Netflix Best Picture push.

films.title: Extraction
films.note: Netflix's inflated viewership claims as a stand-in for the death of transparent numbers.

films.title: Triple Frontier
films.note: Amanda's evidence the Netflix numbers are a lie: nobody she knows watched it despite the reported 63 million.

films.title: Rebuilding Paradise
films.note: The National Geographic verite doc following residents of Paradise, CA after the 2018 Camp Fire. The film he's promoting.

films.title: Made in America
films.note: His first documentary, on Jay-Z's Philadelphia festival; a Robert Altman's-Nashville tapestry approach that Jonathan Demme talked him into.

films.title: Rush
films.note: He was in post on it when the Jay-Z doc offer came in.

films.title: Beyond the Mat
films.note: A Brian Grazer wrestling doc Howard pitched in on before directing his own.

films.title: Inside Deep Throat
films.note: The other Grazer doc he helped shepherd.

films.title: The Beatles: Eight Days a Week - The Touring Years
films.note: Limited by design to the touring years, which he cracked by thinking of it like Das Boot.

films.title: Pavarotti
films.note: His third music doc, again with producer Nigel Sinclair.

films.title: Das Boot
films.note: The structural model for the Beatles doc: men in a bubble who can't see how the war is going.

films.title: Nashville
films.note: Altman's multi-perspective template for Made in America.

films.title: Backdraft
films.note: He made a fire movie, and says the Paradise footage makes it 'nothing.'

films.title: Hillbilly Elegy
films.note: He cut it in a room next door to Paradise, borrowing a doc-editing trick to fix a scene.

films.title: How the Grinch Stole Christmas
films.note: His litmus-test example: he did it mostly to watch Jim Carrey build the character in the flesh.

films: Once Upon a Time in Hollywood

The French Dispatch.title: The French Dispatch of the Liberty, Kansas Evening Sun

The Beatles: Eight Days a Week - The Tou.title: The Beatles: Eight Days a Week - The Touring Years

Once Upon a Time in Hollywood.title: Once Upon a Time... in Hollywood
